inline_comments.message: Maybe better name will be generateCertificate ?
Why PKIErrorException and IOException was not hidden by generic CmpClientException?
pls see my comments from previous patch. There was some problem with builds and I fixed project structure and applied changes from master. Now build works correctly.
it should be caName - java naming convention
it should be caProfile - java naming convention
This method is not implemented. Pls remove it or implement.

I think that generateCertificate may give the wrong impression - the methods function is to create and send a certificate request and receive and validate the response and return the cert, not to generate the certificate entirely within itself. What do you think?

CmpClientException is for generic errors that occur with the cmpv2Client, however the PkiErrorException is for handling errors that occur because of the pkiResponse we receive from the EJBCA server, while the IOException is for any errors that occur due to the connection to the CA failing, or the url being incorrect etc.
its been removed, so no longer a problem :)

this code I'm pushing up will handle putting together the certificate request, sending it to the ejbca server and recieving the response. However I'm currently working on the code that will validate the response (and handle errors that may occur) and will extract the cert etc. from the response.

So right now the code can't return the certificate, but I wish to still have the structure in place for it to return the cert once it can - so right now its returning null.

1. Can we change method name to createCertificate?
2. We should not use Optional parameters in method
https://dzone.com/articles/using-optional-correctly-is-not-optional

see Item 16
We can skip any corrections in external package, because in the future we will remove this package ...

1. Sure, I'll change the name.
2. I'd actually argue that in this case it makes sense to keep the Optional's here, since it tells someone reading the code that null can be a valid value - without the Optional, its easy to assume that there NEEDS to be date values passed through, when the date variables are both entirely optional. what do you think?

in java there is convention that package names should start from lower letter and it should be in singular form.
Pls change from Exceptions to exception.
the same comment as previous exception

it will better if you put private method below public.
It is easier to read code from top to down as a book.

why didn't you pass HttpClient instance by constructor argument?
It will be easier to mock logic and loose coupling between components.
If you will inject HttpClient by constructor then this method will not be needed.
class has only static methods. It is an util class. Util classes should be marked as final and should has private constructor.
Method has too big complexity. Could you pls extract: GeneralName[] createSansGeneralNames(List<String> sansList) and reuse it?
can you move this logic to try section?
you are not closing stream. Use try-autoclosable mechanism
you are not closing stream. Pls use try-autoclosable mechanism

in project we have convention for test names.
should.....When....

the closeableHttpClient has nothing to do with what would actually be needed for constructing the certRequest toward the ejbca server, and is entirely to do with how internally we're sending and receiving the request. It would confuse and add unneeded complexity to the api, when we can simply generate the CloseableHttpClient like this, still making it easy to mock and test. what do you think?

(also check my comment on the optional variables in CmpClient, also wondering on your opinion regarding my response.)

the original cmpv2Client code that you were putting up didn't return the certificate and didn't have code for handling the response from the server in order to extract the certificate either - There's a fairly significant amount of code involved with parsing the response and would cause this already large review to be even bigger, which is why I'm planning on pushing that code up in a separate review. I think for now it has to return a null.
see my comment above, I believe we should leave it like this for api sake.

but from client point of view after calling the method we expect to receive certificate or get an error.
We are not interested what was executed under the hood. Interface is an abstraction layer.

fine, but CmpClient is an interface (abstraction layer) which will be used by client. Interface should has precise methods and exceptions. As I mention in previous comment. As a client we are interested to receive a certificate or abstract exception. In this case CmpClientException should hide other exceptions.
If IOException/PKIErrorException has been thrown by implementation (CmpClientImpl) then CmpClient should throws CmpClientException with proper information. I mean, exception should have information about a reason of IOException/PKIErrorException inside (Exception(message, cause)).

always you can create two separate methods:

In this case dates are optional.
X509Certificate createCertRequest(      String caName,
      String profile,
      CSRMeta csrMeta,
      X509Certificate csr)
      throws CmpClientException

and 

in this case dates are mandatory.
X509Certificate createCertRequest( 
      String caName,
      String profile,
      CSRMeta csrMeta,
      X509Certificate csr,
      Date notBefore,
      Date notAfter)
      throws CmpClientException
this method also should be removed
check the latest patch, the name has been changed to createCertificate
good point, I've refactored it to use only cmpClientException and wrap IOException/PkiErroException.
pls extract versions to master pom.xml as it is done for other dependencies
